title: SpiceJet Extra Seat
full_text:
BOOK
ADD-ONS
DEALS
GIFT CARD
SPICECLUB
SPICESCREEN
CARGO
CHARTER
TRAVEL POLICIES
LOGIN / SIGNUP
Who doesn't like some extra personal space! Now you can book that for yourself, every time you fly with us, with our Extra Seat add-on product.

OWN SPACE
IN THE SKY

PRIORITY
CHECK-IN

PRIORITY
BOARDING

PRIORITY
BAG-OUT

HOW TO BOOK EXTRA SEATS

Extra seats can be added from the seat map page when booking your flight ticket through our website www.spicejet.com or mobile app. As you choose the seat you wish to occupy during your journey, the system will check if there are any vacant seats adjacent to yours. If adjacent seats are available, you will be presented with the option to either book a Private Row consisting of two extra seats or select an adjacent seat. Once you have made your choice, proceed to confirm your payment to finalize the booking of the extra seats.

If you have already booked your flight, simply fill in your details in the next section. Once your flight ticket is retrieved, you will be directed to the add-ons page, where you can select the Extra Seats through the seat map page.

Extra Seats can be booked up to six hours before the scheduled departure time.

ADD EXTRA SEAT TO YOUR BOOKING NOW

* PNR
* E-MAIL/LAST NAME
RETRIEVE BOOKING
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
What is SpiceJet Extra Seats?
SpiceJet Extra Seats is a product offered by SpiceJet that allows passengers to book additional seats on their flight for added comfort and privacy. Passengers can choose to book an extra seat adjacent to their selected seat or a private row with two extra seats.
What are the steps to avail Extra Seats?
When booking your flight through www.spicejet.com, follow these simple steps to avail of extra seats:
Begin by choosing your desired flight on the SpiceJet website
During the seat selection process, pick the seat you wish to occupy during the journey
After selecting your seat, the system will check if there are any vacant seats adjacent to yours. If adjacent seats are available, you will be presented with the option to either book a Private Row consisting of two extra seats or select an adjacent seat
Once you have made your choice, proceed to confirm your payment to finalise the booking of the extra seat
After completing the payment, you will receive an itinerary via e-mail, which will include the details of the extra seats you have booked
Can I avail Extra Seats if I have already booked my flights?
If you have already booked your flight, you can still add extra seats by following these steps:
Go to the Manage Booking page on our website www.spicejet.com at least 6 hours prior to the scheduled departure time of the flight
Click on the “Choose Your Seat” tab on the add-on page and select your preferred seat (if not already selected)
If adjacent seats to your selected seat are available, you can choose a Private Row or an adjacent seat
After confirming the payment, the extra seats will be booked, and you'll receive an updated itinerary via e-mail
How can I avail SpiceJet Extra Seats during the web check-in process for my flights?

You can add extra seats during web check-in, as long as it's at least 6 hours before flight's scheduled departure time. To do this, follow these steps:
Click on the "Choose Your Seat" tab on the add-on page and select your preferred seat (if not already selected)
If there are vacant seats adjacent to your chosen seat, you'll have the option to select either a Private Row (which includes 2 extra seats) or an adjacent seat
After confirming the payment, the extra seats will be booked, and you'll receive an updated itinerary via e-mail
Under what circumstances would I not receive an option to book Extra Seats?
You would not receive the option to book extra seats in the following cases:
If you have not selected/booked a direct SpiceJet flight
If adjacent seats to your preferred seat are not vacant
If you are trying to book extra seats within 6 hours of the scheduled departure of your flight
If you have selected a middle seat
If the Extra Seat inventory is not available on the selected flight
Under what circumstances would I not receive the option to book a private row (2 extra seats)?
You would not receive the option to book a private row in the following cases:
If you've chosen a window seat and the aisle seat in your row is occupied
If you've chosen an aisle seat and the window seat in your row is occupied
Certain seats may not be available for securing extra seats due to the aircraft's configuration
Is additional baggage included in the SpiceJet Extra Seats product?
As a standard product, Extra Seats do not come with any additional check-in or hand baggage allowance. However, SpiceJet may occasionally provide promotional offers related to excess baggage for the Extra Seat product. For the latest offers, please refer to this page.
If I've bought SpiceJet Extra Seats but can't travel, can I transfer them to other passengers in my PNR?
No, extra seats are non-transferrable. The passenger(s) for whom the extra seat is booked must travel on the originally scheduled itinerary.
Is it possible to modify my selected Preferred Seat or SpiceJet Extra Seats after booking?
Once you've chosen your preferred seat or extra seats, any changes will result in forfeiture of the amount already paid. This means that refunds or adjustments will not be available.
What occurs if I need to cancel or modify my flight booking?
SpiceJet Extra Seats are non-refundable and non-convertible to cash. Therefore, the amount paid for extra seats will be forfeited in the event of flight cancellation or modification.
Will the E-ticket or itinerary for my flight booking include the extra seats I purchased?
Yes, the E-ticket/Itinerary would show all the extra seats in the PNR.
Will the purchased extra seats be indicated on my boarding pass?
No, the boarding pass will not display the extra seats. Passengers are advised to carry their E-ticket or Itinerary during travel.
What are the charges of SpiceJet Extra Seats?

The charges for SpiceJet Extra Seats are dynamic, and you can check them while booking or modifying your flight ticket.

Do I need to pay any additional charges for booking SpiceJet extra seats?

No, airport charges or other fees would not be applicable on SpiceJet Extra Seats. GST is applicable for each Extra Seat purchased and would already be included in the price shown for extra seats.

TERMS AND CONDITIONS
SpiceJet Extra Seats can be booked on all direct flights. These are not available for group bookings.
Passengers can purchase SpiceJet Extra Seats up to six (06) hours before the scheduled departure of the flight.
Passengers have the option to purchase a maximum of two (2) adjacent SpiceJet Extra Seats when pre-booking their flight seats.
Passengers who avail the SpiceJet Extra Seat will enjoy complimentary You1st product, which includes priority check-in, priority boarding, and
priority bag-out.
Passengers using this product must carry their itinerary in either physical or electronic form during travel.
Please note that, once purchased, SpiceJet Extra Seats are non-refundable and non-transferable.
SpiceJet Extra Seats can be purchased during booking, through the "Manage Booking" section on www.spicejet.com after booking, or while
performing web check-in.
The availability of SpiceJet Extra Seats is on a first-come, first-served basis.
In the event of modifications or cancellation by the passenger, SpiceJet Extra Seats will also be cancelled, and other passengers on the same PNR (if any) will not have access to the SpiceJet Extra Seats.
If there is a promotion offering extra baggage with the purchase of extra seats, the following terms and conditions will apply:
Passengers who choose the Extra Seat option will receive an extra 5 kg. baggage allowance in addition to their standard free baggage allowance.i.e. total baggage allowance of 20Kgs (1 pc only) including the additional allowance of 5 kgs .
Passengers who opt for the Private Row option will receive an extra 10 kg. baggage allowance in addition to their standard free baggage allowance i.e. total baggage allowance of 25Kgs (1 pc only) including the additional allowance of 10 kgs.
Extra Baggage offer is valid for booking and travel till June 30, 2026.
Through this promotion, customers can enjoy an additional baggage allowance of up to 10 kg by booking either two seats or a private row for a single passenger. This enables them to carry two pieces of checked-in baggage on domestic routes and three pieces of checked-in baggage on international flights
SpiceJet reserves the right to modify, alter, amend, and/or change any and/or all of the terms and conditions at any time without prior notice. SpiceJet also reserves the right to withdraw and/or cancel this offer at any time without prior notice.

title: SpiceJet SpiceLock
full_text:
BOOK
ADD-ONS
DEALS
GIFT CARD
SPICECLUB
SPICESCREEN
CARGO
CHARTER
TRAVEL POLICIES
LOGIN / SIGNUP
Now you can hold the existing fare of your preferred flight starting at just ₹99 for domestic and ₹149 for international flights – and confirm your booking when it suits you the most. Unbelievable, isn’t it? Well it’s true!

NO FARE SURGE

HOLD TIME OF
48 HOURS

LOCK FARE WITH/
WITHOUT NAME(S)

HOW TO BOOK SPICELOCK

SpiceLock can be added while booking your flight ticket through our website www.spicejet.com and our mobile app after selecting the "Hold Fare Now" option on the flight search page. You can choose to lock your fare with or without passenger names.

Once you've selected your fare, it will be held for 48 hours without any fare surge, allowing you time to make a decision about your confirmed travel.

Please note that SpiceLock is available for flights with departure dates that are 7 days or later for domestic flights and 15 days or later for international flights.

If you have already purchased SpiceLock and are now ready to confirm your travel, please fill in the details in the next section.

CONFIRM YOUR BOOKING NOW

* PNR
* E-MAIL/LAST NAME
RETRIEVE BOOKING
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
How can I lock my fare with SpiceLock?
SpiceLock can be added when selecting the "Hold Fare Now" option on the flight search page while booking your flight ticket through our website www.spicejet.com and our mobile app. You can choose to lock your fare with or without passenger names.
Can I hold my fare without name(s)?
Absolutely! SpiceLock gives you the option to hold your fare without the obligation of furnishing passenger name(s). Nevertheless, it is imperative to note that when you are ready to confirm your hold booking, passenger names must be provided to ensure a seamless confirmation process.
What is the fee for SpiceLock?
The fee for SpiceLock is as follows, and it applies per passenger per segment. Please note that the prices mentioned below include GST:
For domestic flights: ₹99 (with name) and ₹199 (without name)
For international flights: ₹149 (with name) and ₹249 (without name)
Is SpiceLock available for special or sales fares?
Unfortunately, the SpiceLock option cannot be used for promotional, sales and special fares.
How can I confirm my hold booking?
To confirm your hold booking, please visit the "Manage Booking" tab on our website or the "My Trips" tab on our mobile app within 48 hours of holding the fare. Your booking will be confirmed upon successful payment completion.
Can I change the name in the hold booking?
Regrettably, name changes are not permitted in the hold bookings.
Can I add more passengers while confirming my hold-booking?
Unfortunately, it is not possible to add more passengers while confirming a hold-booking. The number of passengers when the fare was initially held, must remain the same during booking confirmation.
I have a hold booking for three passengers, but I only want to confirm the booking for two passengers. How can I proceed?
To confirm the booking for the desired number of passengers, simply uncheck the box next to the passenger(s) you do not wish to include. By doing so, the booking will be confirmed only for the passenger(s) that remain checked.
Is it possible to make changes to a held booking?
Unfortunately, modifications cannot be made to a held booking.
What happens if there is a change in the flight schedule while the fare is being held?
In the event that the flight schedule changes within the hold period, with a preponed departure of at least 1 hour or a delay of 2 hours or more, you have two options: you can choose to accept the changes and proceed with booking at the locked fare or you may request a refund of the SpiceLock fee by contacting us via email at custrelations@spicejet.com.
If I fail to confirm my booking within 48 hours, what will be the status of my held fare?
If you fail to confirm your booking within the 48-hour period, your held fare will be released, and the SpiceLock fee will be forfeited.
Is the SpiceLock fee refundable?
The SpiceLock fee is non-refundable, except in the case of IROP (Irregular Operations) flights.
Is the SpiceLock fee included in the final ticket cost?

The final ticket cost does not include the SpiceLock fee.

TERMS AND CONDITIONS
SpiceLock is available for all domestic and international bookings made through our website www.spicejet.com and our mobile app.
The fee for SpiceLock is as follows, and it applies per passenger per segment. Please note that the prices mentioned below include GST and will be exclusive of the ﬁnal ticket cost.
For domestic ﬂights: ₹99 (with name) and ₹199 (without name)
For international ﬂights: ₹149 (with name) and ₹249 (without name)
The SpiceLock option is available for tickets booked at least 7 days prior for domestic ﬂight and at least 15 days prior for international ﬂight departures.
Passengers who choose SpiceLock shall have 48 hours from the time of locking the fare to conﬁrm the booking by paying the locked fare amount. If the booking is not conﬁrmed within the 48-hour period, the held fare will be released, and the SpiceLock fee will be forfeited.
The booking can be conﬁrmed upon successful payment completion by visiting the “Manage Booking” tab on our website or the “My Trips” tab on our mobile app within 48 hours of holding the fare.
Until the booking held using SpiceLock is conﬁrmed, modiﬁcations cannot be made. However, once the booking is conﬁrmed, applicable modiﬁcation charges will apply if the customer requests any changes to the conﬁrmed booking.
Passengers opting for SpiceLock without name(s) can furnish passenger names at the time of ﬁnalizing the booking by paying the complete ﬂight ticket cost. Once the name(s) are furnished, they cannot be changed later.
The price for SpiceLock remains the same for via or connecting ﬂights as it is for direct ﬂights.
Payment for the SpiceLock fee cannot be made using Credit Shell, Gift Cards or Spice Loyalty/Spice Bonus Points.
The SpiceLock fee is non-refundable, except in the case of IROP (Irregular Operations) ﬂights.
The SpiceLock option cannot be used for promotional, sales and special fares.
In the event that the ﬂight schedule changes within the hold period, with a preponed departure of at least 1 hour or a delay of 2 hours or more, you have two options: you can choose to accept the changes and proceed with booking at the locked fare, or you may request a refund of the SpiceLock fee by contacting us via email at custrelations@spicejet.com.
SpiceJet reserves the right to modify, alter, amend and/or change any and/or all of the terms and conditions at any time without prior notice. SpiceJet also reserves the right to withdraw and/or cancel this offer at any time without prior notice.
In the event of any misuse and/or abuse of the respective offer by the customer(s) or travel agent(s) (as applicable), SpiceJet reserves the right to deny the offer and/or cancel the relevant booking(s).
In any event, SpiceJet's maximum liability for any valid claim arising out of this offer is limited to the amount paid by the customer under and in respect of the said offer.
For any mobile wallet-related claims, the customer is required to contact the concerned mobile wallet company. SpiceJet will not entertain any claims related thereto.
For any credit or debit card or net banking-related claims, the customer should contact their respective banks. SpiceJet will not entertain any claims related thereto.
Any person availing the offer is deemed to have accepted all the applicable terms and conditions.
Additionally, kindly be advised that the comprehensive terms of carriage, accessible at https://corporate.spicejet.com/Tnc.aspx, shall be applicable, in tandem with the general disclaimers available at https://corporate.spicejet.com/Disclaimer.aspx.

Retirement is just the beginning of new adventures. Enjoy exclusive discounts and
experience the comfort and warmth of flying with us.

SAVE UP TO 14%
ON BASE FARES

REFUNDABLE
BOOKINGS

HOW TO BOOK A SENIOR CITIZEN DISCOUNT

To book a senior citizen discount on SpiceJet, simply visit our website at
www.spicejet.com or call our reservations centre at +91-124-4983410,
+91-124-7101600. The discount is available for passengers aged 60 years or
older and applies to both one-way and round-trip direct domestic flights.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
What is the Senior Citizen Discount?
The Senior Citizen Discount is a special offer for passengers aged 60 years and above. It allows you to save up to 14% on the base fare when you book your flight directly on our website www.spicejet.com or through our reservation centre.
How can I avail Senior Citizen Discount?
You can use the discount by booking directly on our website,www.spicejet.com, or by calling our reservations centre at +91-124-4983410, +91-124-7101600.
What does the Senior Citizen Discount cover?
The discount applies only to the base fare of your flight ticket. It does not cover additional charges such as taxes, fees, and other surcharges, which will be levied in full at the time of booking.
Is the Senior Citizen Discount valid for international flights?
Unfortunately, no. The Senior Citizen Discount is currently available only for direct domestic flights.
Can I make changes or cancel my booking with the Senior Citizen Discount?
Yes, you can make changes to your booking or cancel it altogether with applicable charges. However, keep in mind that the booking cannot be transferred to another person, and you cannot change the name on the ticket after it has been booked with the discount applied.
Do I need to carry any documents while checking in at the airport?
Yes, you must carry a valid ID card clearly displaying your date of birth. You will need to present this ID at the check-in counter on the day of your flight. If you fail to produce a valid ID card, you may be required to pay the full fare or even be denied boarding.
TERMS AND CONDITIONS
SpiceJet offers a Senior Citizen Discount of up to 14% on the base fare for passengers aged 60 years and above, while all applicable charges, fees, and taxes remain payable by the passenger.
The Senior Citizen Discount is applicable to direct one-way or round-trip flights within India and can only be booked online at www.spicejet.com or through the Reservation Centre by selecting this option.
Limited seats are available under this discount and are offered on a first-come, first-served basis.
Bookings made with the Senior Citizen Discount can be changed or refunded with applicable charges, but they cannot be transferred to another person. Name changes are also not permitted.
Seniors must present a valid ID with date of birth at check-in; failure to do so may result in full fare charges or denied boarding.
This discount cannot be combined with any other offers, promotions, or discounts.
SpiceJet reserves the right to modify, cancel, or withdraw this discount at any time without prior notice.
SpiceJet's decision on any matter related to this discount will be final and binding.
Additionally, kindly be advised that the comprehensive terms of carriage, accessible at https://corporate.spicejet.com/Tnc.aspx, shall be applicable, in tandem with the general disclaimers available at https://corporate.spicejet.com/Disclaimer.aspx.

title: SpiceJet Armed Forces Discount
full_text:
BOOK
ADD-ONS
DEALS
GIFT CARD
SPICECLUB
SPICESCREEN
CARGO
CHARTER
TRAVEL POLICIES
LOGIN / SIGNUP
To our heroes with gratitude – Enjoy flexible, changeable,
and refundable bookings as you travel with pride and dignity.
Thank you for your service!

UP TO 21% DISCOUNT
ON BASE FARE

CHANGEABLE &
REFUNDABLE BOOKINGS

HOW TO BOOK INDIAN ARMED FORCES DISCOUNT

The Indian Armed Forces discount can be availed when booking your flight via the
SpiceJet website, mobile app, reservation centre, select airport ticketing counters, or
authorised travel agency portals. While searching for your preferred flight, you will see
the ‘Armed Forces’ option displayed on the homepage.

This discount is available to serving J&K Police (excluding their families), retired Indian
Armed Forces, paramilitary personnel, and their families. Please note that the discount
applies only to domestic direct flights.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
Who is eligible for the Indian Armed Forces discount?
The Indian Armed Forces discount is available for active and retired members of the Indian Armed Forces and paramilitary personnel along with their families. It also extends to serving J&K Police (excluding their families).
How much is the discount under the Indian Armed Forces bookings?
SpiceJet offers up to a 21% discount on the base fare for eligible bookings. Passenger Service Fees and relevant airport charges still apply.
Can I combine this discount with other promotional offers?
No, this discount cannot be combined with other promotional offers. If another discount is available, the total discount will be limited to 21%.
Where can I book tickets to avail of this discount?
You can book tickets through SpiceJet’s website, mobile app, reservation centre, select airport ticketing counters, and authorised travel agency portals. The discount is available at airport counters in specific cities, including Jammu, Amritsar, Goa, Agartala, Srinagar, Dehradun, Bagdogra, Guwahati, Port Blair, Vishakhapatnam, and Jabalpur.
Is the discount valid for both one-way and round-trip bookings?
Yes, the discount applies to both one-way and round-trip bookings on domestic direct flights only.
Are bookings made with this discount changeable and refundable?
Yes, bookings made with the Indian Armed Forces discount are changeable and refundable. However, they are not transferable, meaning name changes are not permitted.
Do I need any special documents to carry for check-in?
Yes, passengers must present a valid defence or dependent ID card at the check-in counter and boarding gate to verify eligibility. Failure to show a valid ID may result in additional charges or denial of boarding.
Are there any restrictions on the fare?
Some fares may have restrictions regarding changes or cancellations. If you prefer to avoid these restrictions, you can book separate PNRs for each sector without the benefits or restrictions of this fare.
What if there are no discounted seats available on my preferred flight?
The Indian Armed Forces discount is available on a limited number of seats per flight on a first-come, first-served basis. If no discounted seats are available, you may choose an alternate travel date or book a standard fare.
TERMS AND CONDITIONS
The Indian Armed Forces discount is available to serving J&K Police (excluding their families) and retired Indian Armed Forces and paramilitary personnel along with their families.
SpiceJet provides up to 21% discount on the base fare for Indian Armed Forces bookings. Passenger Service Fee and relevant airport charges are additional and apply to all bookings.
The Indian Armed Forces discount is valid for bookings made through the SpiceJet website, mobile app, reservation centre, select airport ticketing counters listed below, and authorised travel agency portals.
The discount applies to airport ticketing counters in Jammu, Amritsar, Goa, Agartala, Srinagar, Dehradun, Bagdogra, Guwahati, Port Blair, Vishakhapatnam, and Jabalpur.
This discount cannot be combined with other promotional schemes or discounts. If another promotion is available, the discount will be adjusted to ensure the total discount does not exceed 21%.
The discount applies to both one-way and round-trip bookings on domestic direct flights only.
Bookings made with the Indian Armed Forces discount are changeable and refundable, but are not transferable (name changes are not permitted).
Passengers must present a valid defence or dependent ID card at the check-in counter and boarding gate. If a valid ID is not presented, passengers will need to pay applicable charges or boarding will be denied.
Certain fares may have restrictions on changes or cancellations. To avoid these restrictions, passengers can book separate PNRs for each sector without the benefits/restrictions of this fare.
A limited number of seats are available under this discount per flight, on a first-come, first-served basis. If unavailable, passengers may select another travel date or book a standard fare.
Additionally, kindly be advised that the comprehensive terms of carriage, accessible at https://corporate.spicejet.com/Tnc.aspx, shall be applicable in tandem with the general disclaimers available at https://corporate.spicejet.com/Disclaimer.aspx.

HOW TO BOOK GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEE FARE

The Government Employee Fare can be booked while purchasing a new flight ticket through our website www.spicejet.com or via our mobile app. When searching for your desired flight, you will find the Government Employee Fare option on the home page.

Alternatively, you can seek assistance from our reservation centre, visit our airport ticketing counter in person, or utilise the services of authorised travel agents.

The Government Employee Fare is applicable for all domestic direct flights. To book under this category, passengers are required to provide their official government ID.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
What benefits does the Government Employee Fare offer ?
Bookings made under the Government Employee Fare include the following benefits:
Complimentary seat (Non-SpiceMax)
Complimentary meal
Zero convenience fee
Flat fee of ₹500 for changes or cancellations of bookings
50% discount on You1st (You1st is a product that offers priority services for check-in, boarding and baggage delivery)
25% discount on SpiceMax (SpiceMax is a product that offers seat with more legroom, priority services and meal with beverage)
Hold time of 6 hours for bookings made at least 72 hours in advance
What is the maximum number of seats I can purchase under this fare?
The availability of seats is limited and operates on a first-come, first-served basis.
How do I select my complimentary meal and seat with the Government Employee Fare?
During the booking process, go to the Add-ons page, where available meals and seats will appear at zero cost. Simply select your complimentary meal and preferred seat before proceeding to the payment page.
What happens if I don’t select my meal and seat on the Add-ons page?
If you do not select a meal and seat on the Add-ons page, an error message will appear when you attempt to proceed to payment, prompting you to make your selections. The booking will not proceed until the complimentary meal and seat have been selected.
Can I still get a complimentary meal if I book within 6 hours of departure?
For Government Employee Fare bookings made within 6 hours of departure, the option to select a meal will be unavailable. In these cases, passengers are entitled to one eatable item from the available onboard option.
Can my family travel using the Government Employee Fare?
Certainly, family members of a government employee are eligible to enjoy the benefits of booking under the Government Employee Fare when the employee is transferred to a different location/city. If family members are traveling independently, they must always carry a photocopy of the official ID card of the government employee.
What information should family members provide in the Government ID section on the contact details page while booking the ticket?
Family members can enter the details of the government official's employee ID in the corresponding fields on the contact details page while booking the ticket.
Which document must I present to travel under this category?
You are required to show your government employee ID card when checking in at the airport counter.
How can I purchase tickets under the Government Employee fare?
You can visit www.spicejet.com, select the Government Employee Fare on the home page while searching for your desired flight and proceed with the booking process. Alternatively, you can seek assistance from our reservation centre, visit our airport ticketing counter in person, or utilise the services of authorised travel agents.
Is the Government Employee Fare applicable to all flights within the SpiceJet network?
The Government Employee fare is only available for domestic direct flights.
How can I obtain a Travel Certificate for reimbursement of my travel for a flight booked under the Government Employee Fare?
The Travel Certificate, serving as proof of travel, is available at a charge of ₹300 per person per PNR, inclusive of GST. To request the travel certificate, please visit the link of the Travel Certificate page. Alternatively, you may also email us at custrelations@spicejet.com .
TERMS AND CONDITIONS
The booking(s) under the category of Government Employee Official Travel (hereinafter 'GEOT') can only be made online at SpiceJet Limited's (hereinafter 'SpiceJet') website, i.e., www.spicejet.com, by opting for the category 'Government Employee Official Travel'.
This facility is extended to the employees of the state government and central government, PSUs, employees of public sector banks, employees of educational institutions recognized by the state government, or those who are part of any university or educational board recognized by the central or state government.
The benefits of booking under the GEOT category can also be availed of by family members of a government employee in the event of the transfer of such employee to a different location or city.
Bookings made under the Government Employee Fare include a complimentary meal and the passenger’s choice of seat, subject to availability. Passengers must select their preferred meal and seat on the Add-ons page during the booking process before proceeding to payment. For bookings made within 6 hours of departure (D-6 bookings), meal selection will not be available. In these cases, passengers are entitled to one eatable item from the available onboard option. Seat and meal choices are both subject to availability.
Passengers are required to carry their 'official ID card' of the government institution that they are part of. In the event that the family members of any government employee who have made the booking under this GEOT category are traveling alone, such passengers shall at all times carry a photocopy of the official ID card of the person employed in the government organization. Passengers not in possession of the above-mentioned valid official government ID shall not be accepted for check-in or boarding.
The booking GEOT category can only be made for domestic sectors and direct flights.
The bookings made under this category are non-transferable, and changes in the name of the passenger are not permissible.
A flat fee of ₹500 per passenger per flight shall be levied for cancellation or modification of the bookings made under this category. In case of any modification in the booking under this category, the applicable fare difference shall be paid by the passenger.
The inventory under the GEOT category is limited and is served on a first-come, first-served basis. In the event the customer cannot find inventory under this category, he or she may choose to travel on a different date when the inventory is available or can choose the normal class fare.
SpiceJet reserves the right to modify, alter, amend, and/or change any and/or all of the terms and conditions at any time without prior notice. SpiceJet also reserves the right to withdraw and/or cancel this offer at any time without prior notice.
In the event of any misuse and/or abuse by the customer, SpiceJet reserves the right to deny, withdraw, or cancel the relevant booking(s).
Any person availing of the benefits or making a booking under the GEOT category is deemed to have accepted all the terms and conditions as specified herein.
Any dispute arising out of the offer shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts situated in New Delhi, India.
Additionally, kindly be advised that the comprehensive terms of carriage, accessible at https://corporate.spicejet.com/Tnc.aspx, shall be applicable, in tandem with the general disclaimers available at https://corporate.spicejet.com/Disclaimer.aspx.

HOW TO BOOK FRIENDS AND FAMILY FARE

Friends and Family fare can be selected when searching for your preferred flight on our website, www.spicejet.com,
or by contacting our Reservation Centre.

These fares are applicable for both domestic and international flights, whether they are direct or via routes.
Please note that Friends and Family fares require a booking for a minimum of two (2) passengers and can
accommodate a maximum of nine (9) passengers per reservation.

TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Friends and Family fares are applicable to bookings made for both domestic and international flights, but exclusively for direct and via flights.
These fares can be booked through the official SpiceJet website, www.spicejet.com , or via SpiceJet's Reservation Centre, but are not available for bookings made through the SpiceJet mobile application.
Bookings under these fares are allowed for a minimum of two (2) passengers and a maximum of nine (9) passengers per booking.
Bookings for infants are not eligible for these fares.
Passengers booking under these fares are entitled to a full baggage allowance.
Changes or cancellations for bookings made under these fares can only be processed through SpiceJet's Reservation Centre. Cancellations are permissible only if a minimum of two (2) passengers are retained in the booking.
Additionally, kindly be advised that the comprehensive terms of carriage, accessible at https://corporate.spicejet.com/Tnc.aspx, shall be applicable, in tandem with the general disclaimers available at https://corporate.spicejet.com/Disclaimer.aspx.

FLEET

SpiceJet has recently added to its fleet the B737- MAX 8 aircraft which are newly designed, state-of-art aircraft with the new fuel efficient engines (CFM LEAP 1B)

SpiceJet operates a fleet of Boeing and Q-400s that allow for greater efficiency in maintenance and to support its low-cost structure.

The new-generation Boeing 737-700s, 737-800s and 737-900ERs with winglets have been a conscious choice. These aircraft allow for safe, comfortable and efficient flying and are ideally suited for short to medium-haul flights typical of Indian conditions. Q400s, designed for short haul routes are known for their superlative fuel efficiency and comfort. These aircraft are the most technologically advanced turboprop airliners and are the next step in the continuing evolution of Indian aviation.

PRIVACY POLICY
Introduction

SpiceJet Ltd. ("we," "our," or "us") is committed to protecting and respecting your privacy. This Privacy Notice explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website, https://www.spicejet.com/ . It applies to all personal data collected, used, and stored by us and is in compliance with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

Data Controller and Contact Information

The data controller responsible for your personal information is SpiceJet Ltd, Company Registration Number: L51909DL1984PLC288239, and registered office IGI Airport, Terminal 1D, New Delhi 110037. For any questions regarding this Privacy Notice or data protection matters, you may contact us at:

Email: info@spicejet.com

Phone: +91-124-4983410

Mailing Address: SpiceJet Ltd, 319, Udyog Vihar, Phase IV, Gurgaon, Haryana - 122016

Types of Data We Collect

We collect various types of personal data to provide and improve our services. The types of data we may collect include:

Identity Data: Name, username, or other identifiers you provide.
Contact Data: Email address, phone number, mailing address.
Technical Data: IP address, browser type, time zone settings, location, and other technology on the devices you use to access our website.
Usage Data: Information on how you use our website, such as pages viewed and links clicked.
Marketing and Communications Data: Your preferences for receiving marketing information and communications from us.
How We Collect Your Data

We may collect your data through various methods, including:

Direct Interactions: When you provide your personal information by filling out forms, subscribing to our newsletter, or contacting us directly.
Automated Technologies: As you interact with our website, we may automatically collect Technical and Usage Data about your equipment and browsing behaviour via cookies, server logs, and other similar technologies.
Third Parties or Publicly Available Sources: We may receive personal data about you from third parties such as analytics providers (e.g., Google Analytics) or advertising networks.
Purposes of Data Processing

We use your data for the following purposes:

To provide our services: We process personal data to offer and deliver products, services, and information requested by you.
To manage our relationship with you: This includes notifying you about changes to our Privacy Notice, responding to your inquiries, and providing customer service.
To personalize user experience: We may use data to understand how users interact with our website and tailor content to their interests.
To conduct marketing: With your consent, we may send marketing communications about our services, offers, and promotions.
To maintain security:Your data may be used to monitor and enhance security on our website.
To comply with legal obligations: We may be required to process personal data for compliance with applicable laws and regulatory requirements.
Legal Basis for Processing

We rely on the following legal grounds under the GDPR for processing your data:

Consent: For specific activities where you have provided explicit consent, such as subscribing to newsletters or marketing.
Contractual Necessity: When processing is necessary to provide the services you requested or to fulfil a contract with you.
Legal Obligation: When processing is necessary to comply with legal or regulatory obligations.
Legitimate Interests: For purposes that do not override your privacy rights, such as improving our services, ensuring security, or for direct marketing where appropriate.
Data Sharing and Third-Party Disclosures

We may share your personal data with third parties for specific purposes, including:

Service Providers: We may share your data with third-party service providers who assist us in website operations, analytics, or communications.
Affiliates and Partners: When necessary to provide services or facilitate transactions you have requested.
Legal Authorities: If required by law, we may disclose personal data to law enforcement or regulatory bodies.
International Data Transfers

If we transfer your data outside of the European Economic Area (EEA), we ensure adequate protection of your information. We may rely on approved data transfer mechanisms, such as the EU Standard Contractual Clauses, or ensure the recipient is certified under a recognized data protection framework.

Data Retention Policy

We retain personal data only as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this Privacy Notice, or as required by law. Once your data is no longer necessary, we securely delete or anonymize it.

Data Security Measures

We employ appropriate security measures, including encryption, access controls, and regular security assessments, to protect your personal data from unauthorized access, alteration, or misuse.

Your Rights Under GDPR

As a data subject under GDPR, you have the following rights:

Access: Request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
Rectification: Correct inaccurate or incomplete personal data.
Erasure: Request deletion of your data, subject to certain conditions.
Restriction: Request to limit our processing of your data.
Portability: Obtain a portable copy of your data to transfer to another provider.
Objection: Object to certain processing activities, such as direct marketing.
Withdraw Consent: Where consent is required for processing, you may withdraw it at any time.

To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at info@spicejet.com. We may need to verify your identity before fulfilling requests.

Use of Cookies

Our website uses cookies to enhance user experience and analyse web traffic. For more information on the cookies we use and how you can control them, please refer to our [Cookie Policy/Settings].

Third-Party Links

Our website may contain links to external sites. This Privacy Notice does not cover those sites, and we are not responsible for their privacy practices. We encourage you to review the privacy policies of any third-party sites you visit.

Updates to This Privacy Notice

We may update this Privacy Notice to reflect changes in our practices or legal requirements. We will post any updates on this page, and significant changes may be communicated to you directly.

Complaints

If you are unsatisfied with our handling of your personal data, you may contact us at [Contact Email Address]. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority in your country of residence or where you believe a GDPR infringement has occurred.

Contact Us

For further inquiries regarding this Privacy Notice or our data processing activities, please reach out to:

[Company Name]: SpiceJet Ltd.

[Contact Email Address]: info@spicejet.com

[Contact Phone Number]: +91-124-4983410

And DPDP

Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP) Privacy Notice

Introduction

SpiceJet Ltd ("we," "our," or "us") is committed to protecting your privacy in line with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP) of India. This Privacy Notice explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect personal data on our website, https://www.spicejet.com/.

Data Fiduciary

SpiceJet Ltd, Company Registration Number: L51909DL1984PLC288239, and registered office IGI Airport, Terminal 1D, New Delhi 110037. acts as the Data Fiduciary responsible for processing your personal data. If you have questions or concerns regarding this Privacy Notice, you can contact us at info@spicejet.com.

Types of Data We Collect

We may collect various categories of personal data, including:

Identity Data: such as name, age, or any other identifiers.
Contact Data: including email address, phone number, and mailing address.
Technical Data: IP address, browser type, device information, and other technical information collected through cookies or similar technologies.
Usage Data: details about how you interact with our website and the services you use.
Preferences: your preferences for communication and marketing.
How We Collect Your Data

We collect personal data from:

Direct Interactions: Information you provide when filling out forms, subscribing to newsletters, or contacting us directly.
Automated Technologies: Information collected automatically as you interact with our website, including cookies and similar tracking technologies.
Third Parties: We may receive personal data from analytics providers, advertising networks, or other partners.
Purposes of Processing Your Data

We process your data to:

Provide and Improve Our Services: To offer services requested by you and improve user experience.
Communicate with You: To respond to your inquiries and communicate updates, offers, or information.
Analyse and Personalize: To analyse website usage and personalize your experience.
Marketing: With your consent, to send marketing messages and special offers.
Security and Compliance: To maintain website security and comply with legal obligations.
Legal Basis for Processing

Under the DPDP Act, we process your personal data based on the following grounds:

Consent: We obtain your explicit consent for certain types of data processing, especially for marketing and analytics.
Necessary Processing: Where processing is essential to fulfil your request or for legitimate purposes of our business.
Compliance with Legal Obligations: When required to comply with applicable laws and regulations.
Data Sharing and Third-Party Access

We may share your data with:

Service Providers: Third-party providers that help us operate and maintain our website.
Business Partners: To facilitate services or transactions you have requested.
Legal Authorities: If required to comply with legal obligations or protect our rights.
International Data Transfers

If your data is transferred outside India, we ensure adequate safeguards are in place as required by the DPDP Act to protect your privacy.

Data Retention

We retain your personal data only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this notice, or as required by law. Afterward, your data will be securely deleted or anonymized.

Data Security Measures

We implement appropriate measures, such as encryption, secure servers, and access controls, to protect your personal data from unauthorized access or misuse.

Your Rights Under the DPDP Act

As per the DPDP Act, you have rights regarding your data, including:

Right to Access: Request a copy of your personal data.
Right to Correction: Correct any inaccurate or outdated information.
Right to Erasure: Request deletion of data under certain circumstances.
Right to Portability: Obtain a copy of your data in a portable format.
Right to Grievance Redressal: Lodge a complaint if you believe your data rights are violated.

To exercise these rights, please info@spicejet.com

Use of Cookies

Our website uses cookies to improve functionality and analyse usage. For details on the cookies we use, please see our [Cookie Policy/Settings].

Third-Party Links

Our website may contain links to external websites. This Privacy Notice does not cover those websites, and we are not responsible for their privacy practices. We encourage you to read the privacy notices of any third-party sites you visit.

Updates to This Privacy Notice

We may periodically update this Privacy Notice to reflect legal requirements or changes in our practices. We will post any updates on this page, and significant changes may be communicated directly to you.

Contact Us

If you have questions or need further information regarding this Privacy Notice, please contact us:

SpiceJet Ltd

Email: info@spicejet.com

Phone: +91-124-4983410

Mailing Address: SpiceJet Ltd, 319, Udyog Vihar, Phase IV, Gurgaon, Haryana - 122016

Family and Friends discount is applicable for a minimum of 2 passengers (excluding infant) on a single booking. Terms and conditions apply.
The special discounted fares are applicable to senior citizens, above the age of 60 years. To travel under this fare, the passenger must present a valid age proof at the SpiceJet check-in counter at the airport.
Children between the ages of 5 and 12 travelling alone are considered unaccompanied minors. Charges per minor for each domestic sector are INR 4999, and charges vary for each international sector, starting at INR 9999. This can be bought until 2hrs/3hrs (domestic/international) prior to departure. After booking, please fill out the Unaccompanied Minor Form and carry four printed copies to the airport.
Applicable for all students above the age of 12 years studying with a bonafide school/university. Limited inventory available. Valid photo ID and educational institute I-card need to be presented for verification at check-in. Bookings under this offer are refundable. Conditions apply.
Applicable for all serving and retired Indian Armed Forces personnel, Paramilitary Forces personnel and their dependent families. Limited inventory available. Valid photo ID and relevant military card need to be presented for verification at check-in. Conditions apply.
The fares are extended only to government officials for their official travel (those carrying a valid identity card). A valid identity card issued by the employer is a pre-requisite for travel under this offer and passengers will be required to produce the card at the SpiceJet check-in counter at the airport.

title: SpiceJet Fitness to Fly Guidelines
full_text:
[Page 1]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines

[Page 2]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 2 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
INTENTIONALLY LEFT BLANK

[Page 3]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 3 
 
Introduction 
Air travel has some unique features as compared to any other mode of transportation. These factors must be taken into consideration by 
passengers with medical conditions to ensure they have a safe and comfortable journey. 
For the fit, healthy and mobile individual, there is usually no problem but for the passenger with certain pre-existing conditions or those who 
have chances of developing an acute medical problem in flight, the cabin environment may further deteriorate the condition. Medical 
emergencies in an aircraft can be due to worsening of a pre-existing medical condition or an acute event occurring in a previously healthy 
individual. Although the main problems relate to lack of oxygen and expansion of trapped gases, the stress and physical challenge to passengers 
of the complex airport environment also can become a factor. (E.g. carrying bags, walking long distances and dealing with unexpected delays). 
Important factors to be considered by the passenger and the treating medical practitioner before taking a flight are: 
 The amount of oxygen in an aircraft is less as compared to ground levels as the aircraft is pressurised to a level that is equivalent to being up to 
8,000 ft. altitude. Passengers with anaemia, or heart and lung conditions may be at risk and supplemental oxygen may be required or travel be 
delayed until their condition has improved. Severe or complex cases may need to have altitude simulation studies to ensure their safety during 
the flight. 
 Access to advanced medical care in flight may not be possible in an aircraft. Cabin Crew receive training in first aid and basic life support and in 
the use of emergency medical equipments, including Automated External Defibrillators (AED). However limited space in an aircraft may pose 
serious problems while instituting even basic medical care on-board. Emergency landings/ diversions are very complex and may not be always 
possible. Cabin crew will not be able to provide passengers with any assistance for feeding, elimination functions including assistance inside the 
lavatory or other personal care needs. 
 Medical kits are available on-board, but the cabin crew can only open them in the presence of a registered medical practitioner. However 
advanced medical care on-board is not possible. Passengers with medical conditions that carry a high risk of requiring extraordinary medical 
assistance in flight may not be accepted for air travel.

[Page 4]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 4 
 
A Medical Information Form (MEDIF) should be submitted if the passenger has any of the following:  
 An injury, illness or medical condition that may cause a significant problem for self or for others in flight (see table below) - E.g. active heart 
disease/angina, severe mobility problems, psychiatric problems, injury and unable to bend the knee. 
 A medical condition that may be made worse by the flight itself - E.g. significant lung disease, middle ear and sinus problems, recent surgery. 
 An infectious disease that could be contagious at the time of travel - E.g. chicken pox, TB, measles, mumps, influenza. 
 A requirement for special medical equipment - E.g. oxygen, POC, stretcher 
 Consider a MEDIF for passengers with an obvious medical condition that may cause difficulties or challenges during boarding (e.g. new limb 
casts, resolving chicken pox). Consider continence, mobility and comfort of other passengers. Please advice about recent exacerbations or 
complications of chronic conditions. See the table below for further details on specific conditions.  
 There is no need to complete a MEDIF for mobility problems requiring only a wheelchair to the aircraft door, visually impaired, hearing 
impaired or for uncomplicated singleton pregnancy travelling before the 36th week (see below for further details).  
 
Note: MEDIF should be submitted 48 hours prior to travel and approval for travel should be given by the SpiceJet Medical team. It is advisable to brief the 
Reservation staff/ booking agent regarding any medical conditions at the time of booking tickets.  
Do contact SpiceJet Medical Department for any further details and clarifications on the concerned subject matter. 
 
(Contact: custrelations@spicejet.com)

[Page 5]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 5 
 
Condition 
Not Fit to Fly 
Fit to Fly 
Remarks 
Cardiovascular Conditions 
Angina 
Unstable angina or angina with 
minimal exertion 
Controlled with medicines. No Angina 
at rest. 
Passenger should carry his/her own 
medicines in hand baggage in case of 
an angina attack. 
Myocardial Infarction (MI) 
Episode in last 10 days or high 
risk (EF less than 40%), heart 
failure, revascularization.  
 More than 10 days if uncomplicated 
MI.    
 In case of complication 10 days from 
the day complication resolves. 
Complications can be- Any cardiac 
failure     
 Any arrhythmia 
 Any post-MI angina pain 
 Is the heart size larger? 
 Any pre-attack angina 
Cardiac failure 
Acute heart failure or 
uncontrolled chronic heart 
failure. 
If cardiac failure is controlled and 
patient is stable. 
Adequate control means someone 
who can walk 50 meters or go up a 
flight of stairs on room air at a normal 
pace without developing 
breathlessness. Otherwise, in-flight 
oxygen needs to be provided to avoid 
serious complications due to hypoxia.  
Pulmonary edema 
Unresolved. 
Resolved pulmonary edema and any 
precipitating condition should also 
have been resolved. 
May also need to comply with 
myocardial infarction rules if the 
same is associated with pulmonary 
edema.

[Page 6]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 6 
 
Condition 
Not Fit to Fly 
Fit to Fly 
Remarks 
Cyanotic congenital heart 
diseases 
All cases. 
Accepted only if MEDA clearance 
obtained and Inflight oxygen is 
arranged for all cases, after thorough 
medical assessment report. 
  
Cardiac surgery 
10 days or less for CABG and 
valve surgery. Recent 
transpositions, ASD, VSD, 
transplants etc. 
 After 10 days, if no complications and 
medically stable. 
ASD- Atrial septum defect. 
VSD- Ventricular septum defect. 
CABG- Coronary Artery Bypass Graft.  
Angiography 
24 hours or less. 
 More or equal to 24 hours if original 
condition is stable. 
  
Angioplasty with or 
without stent (Widening 
of arteries) 
3 days  
After 3 days, if asymptomatic. 
  
Serious cardiac 
arrhythmia 
Within 7 days. 
More than 7 days if hemodynamically 
stable and no other complication, 
precipitating cause or condition is 
identified, treated and is under 
control. 
Does not include benign arrhythmias. 
Pacemaker or Defibrillator 
implantation (Initial 
implantation / insertion) 
Within 2 days 
More or equal to 2 days if no 
pneumothorax and heart rhythm is 
stable. 
After initial insertion of pacemaker 
and once stable on pacemaker 
passenger may require facilitation 
through airport security checks / 
avoidance of metal detectors 
depending on manufacturer’s 
specifications.

[Page 7]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 7 
 
Condition 
Not Fit to Fly 
Fit to Fly 
Remarks 
Ablation therapy 
Within first 2 days 
More or equal to 2 days if no 
complications and asymptomatic. 
Passenger flying within one week of 
the ablation therapy, procedure is 
considered as high risk of DVT and 
hence if accepted, should be advised 
precautions for DVT or if required 
may be started on medical treatment 
for prevention of DVT. 
Deep Vein Thrombosis 
(DVT) 
If active 
Once asymptomatic and no 
complications. 
Should be stable on oral 
anticoagulants. All precautions to be 
advised to prevent DVT. 
Pulmonary embolism
Within 4 days of onset
5 days or more – If anticoagulation is 
stable and PAO2 normal on room air. 
The new direct factor Xa inhibitor may 
be acceptable. 
Hypertension
Uncontrolled high blood 
pressure. 
Controlled under treatment
Respiratory Conditions 
Pneumothorax (Air in the 
cavity of lungs due to a 
puncture wound or 
spontaneous) 
6 days or less after full inflation, 
if general condition is adequate, 
early transportation with 
“Heimlich type” drain and a 
doctor or nurse escort, only is 
acceptable 
 7 days after full inflation  
 14 days after full inflation for 
traumatic pneumothorax 
 May not travel until 14 days after 
full lung inflation occurred if 
managed closed 
 If chest drain in-situ, may travel 
with medical escort at any time if 
other conditions permit and spare 
drain are carried

[Page 8]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 8 
 
Condition 
Not Fit to Fly 
Fit to Fly 
Remarks 
Chest surgery
10 days or less
More or equal to 11 days with 
uncomplicated recovery. 
e.g. lobectomy, pleurectomy, open 
lung biopsy etc. 
Pneumonia 
Acute conditions and with 
symptoms 
Fully resolved OR if chest x-ray signs 
persists then passenger must be 
symptom free and in non-infective 
stage of the disease. 
 
COPD, emphysema, 
pulmonary fibrosis, 
pleural effusion and 
haemothorax 
 If unstable or poor exercise 
tolerance 
 Within 7 days of recent 
exacerbation 
 Cyanosis on the ground 
despite supplementary 
oxygen 
 Unresolved recent 
exacerbation. 
 Exercise tolerance (Walking) more 
than 50 meters without dyspnoea 
and general condition is normal. 
 More than 7 days from recent 
exacerbation and full recovery 
from recent exacerbation. 
 No currently active infection. 
 Supplementary oxygen may be 
required based on pulse oximeter 
saturation readings. 
Cystic fibrosis
FEV1 < 50% at ground level.
No current infection. 
Requirement of supplementary 
oxygen on board to be assessed. 
Asthma 
 
 Active asthma attack. 
 < 48 hours after severe 
asthma attack or asthma 
which requires 
hospitalization 
 Asymptomatic and no infection. 
 More than 48 hours after severe 
asthma attack or asthma which 
requires hospitalization, if currently 
asymptomatic and no infection 
Must be stable and carry appropriate 
medication on-board in hand 
baggage.

[Page 9]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 9 
 
Condition 
Not Fit to Fly 
Fit to Fly 
Remarks 
Cancer 
 Under active treatment 
(radiotherapy or 
chemotherapy). 
 With pleural effusion. 
 Dyspnoea at ground level. 
 Major haemoptysis 
 Asymptomatic with acceptable 
general condition and no cyanosis 
or dyspnoea at ground level. 
 Requirement of supplementary 
oxygen on- board flight needs to be 
assessed based on pulse oximeter 
readings. 
Major haemoptysis is a 
contraindication. 
Bronchiectasis 
Hypoxemic at ground level 
 No current infection. 
 Supplementary oxygen 
requirement on -board to be 
assessed based on pulse oximeter 
readings. 
 
Neuromuscular Disease 
 Severe extra pulmonary 
restriction. 
 Need home ventilation. 
 Supplementary oxygen 
requirement on-board to be 
assessed based on pulse oximeter 
readings 
 Medical/ nonmedical escort 
requirement to be assessed based 
on medical assessment. 
 
Pulmonary arteriovenous 
malformations 
 
If severe hypoxemic 
(SpO2 < 80%) at ground level. 
 If not hypoxemic at ground level. 
 No other complications 
Supplementary oxygen on-board will 
be required and should be arranged 
beforehand through MEDA approval 
process.

[Page 10]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 10 
 
Condition 
Not Fit to Fly 
Fit to Fly 
Remarks 
Ventilators
Seriously ill cases
Accepted if long term stable cases 
requiring only ventilation with air 
Acceptance is also subject to 
equipment clearance by Engineering, 
DGR and Security Departments. 
Gastrointestinal conditions 
Gastrointestinal bleeding 
24 hours or less following a 
bleed. 
≥ 10 days only if, 
 Bleeding should have stopped. 
Hemoglobin limits must be met. 
 Risks of re-bleeding must be 
assessed and should be acceptable.
 
Within 1-9 days passenger can travel 
if endoscopic or other clear evidence 
(i.e. Hemoglobin continued to rise to 
indicate bleeding has ceased) of 
healing. 
Major abdominal surgery 
10 days or less. 
More than 10 days if uncomplicated 
recovery. 
 
e.g. bowel resection, ‘open’ 
hysterectomy, renal surgery etc. 
Appendectomy 
4 days or less. 
5 days or more if uncomplicated 
recovery. 
 
  
Laparoscopic 
surgery(Keyhole surgery) 
4 days or less. 
5 days or more if uncomplicated 
recovery. 
 
e.g. cholecystectomy (gall bladder 
removal), tubal surgery etc. 
Investigative laparoscopy 
24 hours or less 
24 hours or more if gas is absorbed 
completely.

[Page 11]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 11 
 
Condition 
Not Fit to Fly 
Fit to Fly 
Remarks 
Colostomy 
 Less than 5 days if simple 
colostomy is performed and 
not associated with any 
major abdominal surgery. 
 Less than 10 days if also had 
major abdominal surgery (i.e. 
bowel resection with 
colostomy etc.) 
 ≥ 5 days if simple uncomplicated 
colostomy. 
 ≥ 10 days if also had major 
abdominal surgery (i.e. bowel 
resection with colostomy etc.) and 
currently no complications. 
 Colostomy must be working, 
patient tolerating oral intake, no 
abdominal distension, no nausea 
or vomiting. 
 Passenger should be able to take 
care of the colostomy opening and 
colostomy bag. 
 If passenger cannot take care of 
the same then passenger will need 
to travel with an escort who can 
take care of the same. 
 
It is essential to empty colostomy 
bag before boarding of the flight 
as colostomy output may slightly 
increase during flight. 
Vomiting, Diarrhoea 
 If actively vomiting with 
symptoms of dehydration. 
 Profuse or bloody diarrhoea 
with symptoms of 
dehydration. 
No dehydration and symptomatically 
better.  
  
Renal conditions 
CAPD (Continuous 
Ambulatory Peritoneal 
Dialysis) 
If clinically unstable and 
haemoglobin level < 9 g/dl, 
unless anaemia is due to some 
chronic long term illness. 
 
If clinically stable and haemoglobin 
level > 9 g/dl. 
 
Assessment needed for 
supplementary oxygen during 
flight. 
Should travel with additional CAPD 
bags to cover unforeseen delays and 
seek advice for airport security 
checks. 
Renal calculus 
 Acute attack of renal colic 
 Blood in urine. 
 If stone has passed/been treated 
 Currently asymptomatic.

[Page 12]
Fitness to Fly Guidelines 
 
SG/MED/VER:0.1 
Page 12 
 
Condition 
Not Fit to Fly 
Fit to Fly 
Remarks 
Blood disorders 
Anaemia 
Hb < 9 g/dl unless anaemia is 
due to some chronic long term 
illness. 
Hb > 9 g/dl 
If acutely anaemic, Hb level should be 
assessed within 24 hours before date 
of travel and blood loss should have 
ceased/stopped. 
Sickle cell disease 
Sickling crisis in previous 10 days ≥ 10 days of sickling crisis(and no 
recurrence of sickling crisis after that) 
Always need supplement of oxygen to 
prevent sickling crisis on flight 
Bleeding disorders 
If active bleeding is present 
When bleeding is controlled 
•  Also follow rules for anaemia (acute 
anaemia) 
• Take care on-board to prevent 
injuries which may restart bleeding. 
Blood clotting disorders, 
Thrombophilia 
Active stage. 
 Stabilized on therapeutic 
anticoagulation for at least 24 hours 
or more since last episode. 
 No side effects (like bleeding) of 
anticoagulation treatment 
  
Infectious diseases 
Infectious diseases 
During contagious/infectious 
stage of illness. 
Must be in non-infectious stages. 
For infectivity periods of some 
common infections 
Measles 
Till 5 days after the onset of 
rashes 
After 5 days of onset of rashes

SG/MED/VER:0.1

Condition 
Not Fit to Fly 
Fit to Fly 
Remarks 
Mumps 
Till 7 days after the onset of 
rashes 
After 7 days of onset of rashes 
 
Rubella 
Till 4 days after the onset of 
rashes 
After 4 days of onset of rashes

 Carriage of CPAP, BiPAP: Carriage of these devices on board is subject to security clearance. 
Additional restrictions apply if the machine is powered by lithium batteries. Please write to custrelations@spicejet.com for clarifications on the 
same. 
As Inflight power supply cannot be provided, the CPAP/BiPAP device must use a battery that can power it for at least 150% of the flight time. 
For instance, if the flight duration is 2 hours long, the battery supply should last for at least 3 hours. 
To check the flight duration and to make the necessary arrangements, please get in touch with our customer relations executive at least 48 
hours before the departure of the flight. 
Passenger must report to the check in counter, at least two hours before the departure time. 
Front row seats and emergency exit row seats will not be assigned for those carrying these devices due to safety reasons.  
 
 Carriage of POC:  Portable oxygen concentrator (POC) device can be carried and used on board our flights as long as it is a model that is 
approved by FAA. Passenger must report to the check-in counter two hours before the departure time so that necessary verification of POC & 
MEDIF can be carried out.

Since the above mentioned POCs are big in size we will be unable to secure them underneath the seat.  
When in use, all POCs must be stowed in the space beneath the seat in front of you.  This is with the exception of Lifestyle/Freestyle 
(manufactured by the AirSep Corporation), which may also be placed on your seat or held by you due to its lightweight and small size. 
For use during flight, your device must fit under the seat in front of you. Additional restrictions apply if your machine is powered by lithium 
batteries. Please write to custrelations@spicejet.com for clarifications on the same.  
As Inflight power supply cannot be provided, the POC device must use a gel or dry battery that can power it for at least 150% of the flight time. 
For instance, if the flight duration is 4 hours long, the battery supply should last for at least 6 hours. 
To check the flight duration and to make the necessary arrangements, please get in touch with our customer relations executive at least 48 
hours before your flight. A duly filled MEDIF from your physician to indicate your fitness to travel should also be submitted.

Pax must report to the check in counter, at least 2 hours before departure of the flight. 
Front row seats and emergency exit row seats will not be assigned for those carrying POC due to safety reasons.  
 
 Insulin Syringes/ hypodermic needles: 
Carriage of these syringes/needles are subject to security clearance. A prescription or note from a registered physician is required to carry 
syringes and/or hypodermic needles on board. Please do not dispose of used syringes or needles in the seat pockets or without the protective 
caps. Our cabin crew will be happy to assist you with the safe disposal of used syringes and/or needles. 
 
 Portable hearing aids, Nebulisers: 
You will be required to operate your own medical equipment, without the assistance of our cabin crew. We will not be able to provide inflight 
power supply on your flights with us. We recommend that you use a gel or dry battery that can power the equipment for at least 150% of the 
flight time. For instance, if your flight is 4 hours long, please have a battery supply of 6 hours. 
 
 Tablet, Capsules, liquid medications and Inhalers:  
Passengers are allowed to bring any medication medically necessary on board their flight with them, whether it is prescription or over-the-
counter medication. All medications should be declared to a SpiceJet officer for screening, even if they are solid pills or inhalers. Medications 
should be preferably labelled, though in practice all pills and capsules are to be kept in their original bottle with prescription if possible.  
It is preferable to declare any liquid prescription over 100 ml at the security checkpoint before your bag is screened. 
 
While no restrictions apply to prescription medications in checked baggage, packing medications in carry-on bag is advisable. Keeping 
medications accessible in a separate bag within the carry-on bag is advisable so that it is easier to remove them during the security screening. 
It is advisable to carry your own Asthma inhaler in your carry on hand baggage with you. We will be able to provide you with Asthalin Inhaler in 
case of need, but only if we have a registered medical practitioner travelling on board the aircraft.
